Cleveland House") * [Coley Building](/wiki/Coley_Building "Coley Building") * [Collins–Marston House](/wiki/Collins%E2%80%93Marston_House "Collins–Marston House") * [Collins–Robinson House](/wiki/Collins%E2%80%93Robinson_House "Collins–Robinson House") * [Convent of M

is a [historic district](/wiki/Historic_district_(United_States) "Historic district (United States)") in the city of [Mobile, Alabama](/wiki/Mobile,_Alabama "Mobile, Alabama"), United States. It was placed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") on February 9, 1979.[[1]](#cite_note-NRIS-1) The district encompasses all of Dauphin Street from Water Street to Jefferson Street.[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2) It covers 551 acres (2.23 km2) and contains 736 contributing buildings. The boundaries were increased on February 19, 1982, June 30, 1995, August 14, 1998, and September 3, 2019. The buildings range in age from the 1820s to the 20th century and include the [Federal](/wiki/Federal_architecture "Federal architecture"), [Greek Revival](/wiki/Greek_Revival_architecture "Greek Revival architecture"), [Queen Anne](/wiki/Queen_Anne_style_architecture_in_the_United_States "Queen Anne style architecture in the United States"), [Italianate](/wiki/Italianate_architecture "Italianate architecture"), and various other [Victorian](/wiki/Victorian_architecture "Victorian architecture") architectural styles.[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2)

The **Lower Dauphin Street Historic District** is a [historic district](/wiki/Historic_district_(United_States) "Historic district (United States)") in the city of [Mobile, Alabama](/wiki/Mobile,_Alabama "Mobile, Alabama"), United States. It was placed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") on February 9, 1979.[[1]](#cite_note-NRIS-1) The district encompasses all of Dauphin Street from Water Street to Jefferson Street.[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2) It covers 551 acres (2.23 km2) and contains 736 contributing buildings. The boundaries were increased on February 19, 1982, June 30, 1995, August 14, 1998, and September 3, 2019. The buildings range in age from the 1820s to the 20th century and include the [Federal](/wiki/Federal_architecture "Federal architecture"), [Greek Revival](/wiki/Greek_Revival_architecture "Greek Revival architecture"), [Queen Anne](/wiki/Queen_Anne_style_architecture_in_the_United_States "Queen Anne style architecture in the United States"), [Italianate](/wiki/Italianate_architecture "Italianate architecture"), and various other [Victorian](/wiki/Victorian_architecture "Victorian architecture") architectural styles.[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2)
